meta.enforced_by: LAW 20-22 — Every service MUST have a defined failure propagation policy

failure_modes.description: Actions available in the failure propagation matrix

RETRY.description: Re-attempt the failed operation with backoff

FALLBACK.description: Use alternative execution path or degraded mode

CIRCUIT_BREAK.description: Open circuit to prevent cascading failures

BUFFER.description: Queue the operation for deferred processing

CONTINUE.description: Proceed without the failed operation's result

DEFER.description: Postpone operation to a later retry cycle

RECORD.description: Log failure for telemetry without blocking execution

CLASSIFY.description: Analyze failure pattern and update FailureIntelligence

RELEASE.description: Release resource lease so others can acquire

NOTIFY.description: Emit event to EventBus for downstream consumers

STRONG.description: Operation must complete synchronously with ACID guarantees

EVENTUAL.description: Operation may complete asynchronously; state converges over time

propagation_matrix.description: Source → Effect → Action for every failure scenario in D8.2

entries.action_sequence:
1. RETRY — Scheduler re-dispatches via run_with_timeout
2. CLASSIFY — RetryHandler records failure pattern
3. RELEASE — LeaseManager releases any held lease
4. NOTIFY — EventBus emits DISPATCH_FAILED

entries.failure: monitor_heartbeat returns False or lease TTL expired
entries.action_sequence:
1. CANCEL — Engine sets cancel flag
2. ROLLBACK — Scheduler rolls back partial execution
3. REASSIGN — LeaseManager marks resource as available
4. RECORD — StateStore persists cancellation trace

1. DEGRADE — Core switches to in-memory-only mode
2. BUFFER — Scheduler buffers results for deferred persist
3. CONTINUE — Execution proceeds without checkpoint
4. DEFER — RetryHandler retries persistence in background

1. FAIL_FAST — Immediately return error
2. RECORD — StateStore persists failure reason
3. NOTIFY — EventBus emits SCHEDULING_FAILED

entries.failure: decide_retry raises RetryDecisionError or max_attempts exhausted
entries.action_sequence:
1. FAIL_FAST — Mark node as permanently failed
2. RELEASE — LeaseManager releases execution lease
3. RECORD — StateStore persists terminal failure
4. NOTIFY — EventBus emits RETRY_EXHAUSTED

1. CANCEL — Scheduler stops dispatching new nodes
2. RELEASE — LeaseManager releases all active leases
3. RECORD — StateStore persists cancellation checkpoint
4. NOTIFY — EventBus emits EXECUTION_CANCELLED

entries.failure: acquire_lease returns None or raises LeaseError
entries.action_sequence:
1. DEFER — Scheduler defers the node to next scheduling cycle
2. NOTIFY — EventBus emits LEASE_UNAVAILABLE

entries.failure: Node tool execution raises unhandled Exception
entries.action_sequence:
1. CLASSIFY — RetryHandler analyzes failure pattern
2. RETRY — Dispatcher re-dispatches if retryable
3. RELEASE — LeaseManager releases lease if terminal
4. RECORD — StateStore persists failure in trace

recovery_policies.description: Standard recovery policies mapped to each service domain

scheduler_recovery.on_failure: RETRY up to 3 times, then FAIL_FAST
scheduler_recovery.circuit_breaker: Open after 3 consecutive failures, reset after 30s

dispatcher_recovery.on_failure: RETRY up to 2 times, then FALLBACK to direct execution
dispatcher_recovery.circuit_breaker: Open after 5 consecutive failures, reset after 60s

retry_handler_recovery.on_failure: FALLBACK to default retry policy (3 attempts, 1s backoff)
retry_handler_recovery.circuit_breaker: Open after 10 consecutive failures, reset after 120s

state_store_recovery.on_failure: DEGRADE to in-memory buffer, DEFER persist
state_store_recovery.circuit_breaker: Open after 3 consecutive failures, reset after 10s

lease_manager_recovery.circuit_breaker: Open after 3 consecutive failures, reset after 15s
